rca.root_cause: A worker node suffered a kernel panic and remounted its filesystem read-only due to underlying disk I/O errors (hardware/cloud volume failure), which forced container restarts. Application logs show unrelated external scan/probing traffic and are not the cause.
recommendation: Immediately cordon and drain the affected node; replace or recreate the instance and underlying volume, then uncordon once healthy.
Investigate the node: review dmesg/kern.log and kubelet logs for I/O errors, run fsck or provider diagnostics, and check SMART/volume health; retire the node if disk health is questionable.
Enable/verify node auto-repair and install Node Problem Detector; add alerts on kernel panics, filesystem remounts (read-only), and disk I/O error rates.
Harden scheduling resilience: use topology spread constraints/pod anti-affinity across nodes/zones and set PodDisruptionBudgets to limit blast radius.
Tune reliability signals: ensure correct readiness/liveness probes and proper terminationGracePeriod so pods are rescheduled quickly after node failures.
Strengthen storage posture: keep critical data on replicated managed PVs; set kubelet eviction thresholds for disk pressure and monitor disk latency/IOPS.
Upgrade and patch OS/kernel and container runtime to versions without known I/O or filesystem bugs; verify stable filesystem/mount options.
Reduce external noise: place the service behind an ingress/WAF with rate limiting and path filters; block direct node access and ensure no Docker API exposure.
